page: Microsoft Azure E4-2ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Eadsv7 server family. It is provisioned with 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU.
For local storage, the server includes a 236 GB local disk and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or onboard GPU hardware.

Given the high memory density relative to the processing cores, this configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. It is suited for relational database hosting, in-memory caching layers, and enterprise applications with moderate compute requirements but high memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure E4-2ads_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with a 236 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks onboard GPU accelerators and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to processing power. It is suited for hosting small-to-medium databases, in-memory caches, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and local storage.
